source.title: Explosion, Molten Salt Eruption, and Fatal Injury at TS USA Liquid Nitriding Facility

context.operator: Techniques Surfaces USA (TS USA), subsidiary of HEF Groupe

event.top_event: Molten salt eruption and steam explosion
actions_taken: Facility shut down for approximately eight months
Protective enclosure installed at oxidizer bath after incident
Chattanooga Fire Department responded and extinguished fires
Employee transported to hospital and received treatment
event.summary: On May 30, 2024, at 8:58 a.m., an explosion occurred at the TS USA liquid nitriding facility in Chattanooga, Tennessee, while employees were processing metal rollers with cavities. Water became trapped in a roller cavity due to a solidified salt plug blocking the drain hole. When the roller was reintroduced into the 800°F oxidizing salt bath, the trapped water boiled and rapidly expanded as steam, creating overpressure that ejected the roller's press fit end. The contact of water with the molten salt caused a steam explosion that erupted approximately 4,500 pounds of molten sodium hydroxide and sodium nitrate from the bath, engulfing a line operator and causing second- and third-degree chemical and thermal burns over 95 percent of his body. The operator died later that day at the hospital. Three other employees suffered minor burns. The eruption ignited multiple fires throughout the facility, resulting in approximately $1.3 million in property damage.
recommendations: Implement physical protective barriers around molten salt baths to isolate employees from hazardous releases
Develop comprehensive safety management system including hazard analysis program, written operating procedures, training program, and incident investigation program
Establish position with specific expertise in safety management systems at each TS USA facility
Include physical protective barriers as standard design for liquid nitriding processes
Develop and implement effective knowledge management program for sharing information across HEF Groupe organization
Develop and implement comprehensive corporate governance program with regular audits and accountability tracking

hazards.name: Water or moisture in process equipment
Cavities and blind holes in metal parts

threats.name: Accumulation of molten salt in roller cavity
Solidification of salt plug blocking drain hole
Retention of water in roller cavity
Reintroduction of water-containing roller into hot salt bath
Boiling and vaporization of trapped water
Ejection of roller press fit end
Contact of water with molten salt

consequences.name: Exposure of personnel to molten salt

controls.name: Hazard analysis for parts with cavities

supporting_text: TS USA did not have an adequate process safety management system in place at its Chattanooga facility
TS USA lacked hazard analyses on processing new parts, on the liquid nitriding line, and for reprocessing parts

Due to the configuration of the cavity and the small size of the drain hole, the rollers still presented an accumulation hazard. This hazard was not recognized by TS USA's management systems and reviewers

controls.name: Operating procedures for nitriding process
controls.barrier_role: Provide guidance on safe operation and part handling

supporting_text: TS USA lacked procedures for reprocessing parts in the oxidizer bath
TS USA employees were unprotected from and unaware of the hazards presented by the nitriding line, entrapment of materials in the parts, and reprocessing the rollers

controls.name: Training on nitriding process hazards
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel understand hazards and safe practices

supporting_text: TS USA did not have training on the hazards present in the nitriding line
TS USA employees were unaware of the hazards presented by the nitriding line, entrapment of materials in the parts, and reprocessing the rollers

controls.name: Verification that water has drained from parts
controls.barrier_role: Prevent water retention in cavities before reintroduction to hot bath

supporting_text: Although some of the water drained from the roller, it stopped draining before the cavity was empty. Due to the lack of any additional water draining from the parts, the plant manager believed that the part only contained solidified oxidizer salt and no water. No further actions, such as turning the rollers over, were taken to verify that there were no other materials in the roller
The plant manager and supervisor attempted to clear the obstruction from the roller using a piece of wire, but they were unsuccessful, which allowed water to remain in the cavity

controls.name: Drain hole design adequate for complete drainage
controls.barrier_role: Ensure molten salt and water can fully drain from cavities

supporting_text: The drain holes were very small—0.03 square inches, or just 0.25 percent of the cross-sectional area of the cavities, which resulted in slow drain times

The CSB concludes that the rollers were not suitable for processing through the liquid nitriding process. The drain holes were not large enough to ensure the rollers fully drained the molten salts, resulting in the salt plug forming at the bottom of the roller's interior

controls.name: Protective barrier around oxidizer bath
controls.barrier_role: Isolate personnel from molten salt eruption

supporting_text: The line operator was standing behind the protective shielding, less than three feet from the opening of the oxidizer bath, while reintroducing the rollers to the bath
An enclosure installed at the TS USA Chattanooga facility after the incident
Implement physical, protective barriers around the molten salt baths that isolate employees from hazardous releases at all locations that perform liquid nitriding

controls.name: Incident investigation and knowledge sharing
controls.barrier_role: Learn from past incidents and prevent recurrence

supporting_text: Before the 2024 fatal eruption in Chattanooga, HEF Groupe had at least three similar incidents involving the processing of parts with accumulation hazards or sealed cavities
TS USA and HEF Groupe did not ensure that these safety incidents were investigated or that lessons learned from events were shared and incorporated across the organization to prevent future incidents
2018 Event in Mexico
2020 Event in France
2023 Event in Chattanooga

controls.name: Corporate governance and knowledge management
controls.barrier_role: Ensure safety information is communicated across organization

supporting_text: HEF Groupe developed hazard analyses for the nitriding process. However, the details of these analyses and the safeguards were not communicated to HEF Groupe's subsidiary companies, including TS USA

HEF Groupe did not ensure that its subsidiaries were following the company's guidance, such as the risk assessments and safety alert letters, developed at the corporate level to ensure the safety of the operations
HEF Groupe did not ensure that the safety knowledge maintained at the corporate level was communicated to its subsidiary companies to promote the safe operation of the liquid nitriding line

controls.name: Process engineer consultation on troubleshooting
controls.barrier_role: Provide expert guidance on safe resolution of operational issues

supporting_text: The plant manager contacted the process engineer who was responsible for the Chattanooga facility—but who actually was located in southeast Georgia—for guidance on how to clear the obstruction
The process engineer recommended reintroducing the rollers directly into the oxidizing bath

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
